summary:
Great! The test passes now. The fix involved ensuring consistent lock ordering by always acquiring the lock associated with the account that has a lexicographically smaller ID first. This eliminates the possibility of deadlocks because all threads will follow the same ordering sequence for acquiring locks.

The needle (the hidden problem) in this case was the potential deadlock in the transfer function due to inconsistent locking order. By modifying the TransferService.java file to implement consistent lock ordering, we eliminated the deadlock scenario.
